_comment: Structural element-root table for the GENERATIVE main-group oxoacid namer
(iupac_namer/perception/fg/maingroup_oxoacids.py).
This is NOT a whole-molecule SMILES->name pin table.  It maps a central
element to its IUPAC acid-root forms; the namer computes the full name
from perceived structural features (oxo count, hydroxy/oxido count,
polynuclear bridge count, peroxy/anion modifiers).  Because every member
of an element family shares these roots, the table generalises to all
members of the family -- adding e.g. a new selenium chain length needs no
new data.
Citations: IUPAC 2013 Blue Book P-67.1.1.1 (mononuclear acid preselected
names) and P-67.2.1 (polynuclear preselected names).  The 'tier' keys map
the count of single-bonded acidic oxygens on the central atom to the
element-specific root; '-ic' (with central oxo) vs '-ous' (without) is the
oxidation-state suffix.  Pnictogens (N, P, As, Sb) use the systematic
-or-/-on-/-in- infix scheme baked into the per-tier roots; chalcogens
(S, Se, Te) and boron use element-specific roots.
tier = number of single-bonded O substituents on the centre
(-OH, [O-], -O- anhydride bridge, -OO- peroxy, and X-X direct bonds
each count as one acidic position for tier purposes).
suffix 'ic' chosen when the centre bears at least one oxo (=O, or a
charge-balancing [O-] on a cationic centre); 'ous' otherwise.
For elements with no lower oxidation state (B) only the 'ic'-form root
is given and it is used regardless of oxo count.
_halogen_comment: Halogens (F, Cl, Br, I) are mono-hydroxy oxoacids whose oxo count equals
the central formal charge: 0 -> hypo-X-ous, 1 -> X-ous, 2 -> X-ic,
3 -> per-X-ic.  P-67.1.2.2 / P-67.1.1.1 preselected names.  Root forms are
